input.headline: Another Canadian joins Gretzky, Crosby as lone NHL teens with 30 points in first 20 games of a season

raw.headline: Another Canadian joins Gretzky, Crosby as lone NHL teens with 30 points in first 20 games of a season

winner: So, this rookie from Canada breaks into the NHL and scores 30 points in his first 20 games—just like Wayne Gretzky and Sidney Crosby. His coach says, "Kid, you're a natural." The player replies, "Actually, I'm a Canadian. Natural is just what we do to your power play.

candidates.text: I told my kid he'd be famous if he scored 30 points in his first 20 games—he replied, "Dad, I'm not even in the NHL.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with relatable parenting scenario, concise and specific to the headline"}

candidates.text: I tried to join the NHL at 15, but they said I wasn't old enough — apparently, being a teenage prodigy with 30 points in 20 games isn't a valid ID.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on age requirements, ties humor to the headline's context with specificity and brevity."}

candidates.text: So, this rookie from Canada breaks into the NHL and scores 30 points in his first 20 games—just like Wayne Gretzky and Sidney Crosby. His coach says, "Kid, you're a natural." The player replies, "Actually, I'm a Canadian. Natural is just what we do to your power play.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with a play on 'natural' and Canadian hockey culture, but the punchline could be tighter for maximum surprise and impact."}

candidates.text: I tried to join the NHL at 16, but my highlight reel was just me chasing after pucks and my dad's terrible hair.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat relatable and has a mild humorous twist with the dad's hair, but it lacks direct connection to the headline and feels a bit generic in its approach."}

candidates.text: A hockey player scored 30 points in his first 20 games, so his coach told him, "You're like Gretzky and Crosby—just don't get too carried away with the puck.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat relevant and uses hockey references, but it's generic and lacks the surprise or specificity needed to be particularly funny."}

candidates.text: The NHL is officially transitioning into a high school cafeteria where the only sport is power-playing for juice boxes.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the cafeteria metaphor, but it's a bit forced and lacks direct connection to the headline's specific context about Canadian players and points in the NHL."}

candidates.text: The NHL is finally making progress on child labor laws by ensuring every teenager on the ice has at least 30 points before they're allowed to start their shift.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the headline, but the reference to child labor laws feels forced and slightly off-topic, reducing its overall humor impact."}

candidates.text: He’s making a point, but he’s still just a minor league talent.
This kid is scoring so fast he's got a better first-period than a hockey goalie's confidence!
